Event: Bachmanity purchases Pied Piper, making Big Head one of the company's owners
Explanation: Acquisition gives Big Head major ownership benefit while not described as harming others.

Event: Interviewer nearly laughs Big Head out of the office after seeing his qualifications
Explanation: Interviewer protects institutional standards and prestige while briefly humiliating Big Head.

Event: Interviewer decides to talk more with Big Head after hearing he was on Wired
Explanation: Interviewer pursues a promising high-profile candidate, benefiting both Big Head and school.

Event: Big Head goes to his first class assuming he is a student
Explanation: Big Head’s confusion mildly harms his own expectations while providing some novelty/interest to others.

Event: Big Head becomes president of Stanford
Explanation: High-status role greatly benefits him and presumably advances the institution.

Event: Gavin positions Big Head as a fake ultimate hero to promote Nucleus
Explanation: Boosts Gavin’s project at the cost of misleading the public and misrepresenting Big Head.

Event: Big Head’s reputation as a respected public figure persists despite his incompetence
Explanation: Big Head gains ongoing status while others are misled about his actual abilities.

Event: Big Head’s mild-mannered honesty convinces the judge he created Pied Piper’s algorithm
Explanation: His honesty inadvertently benefits him with credit and leverage while disadvantaging the true creators.

Event: Big Head buys a huge house
Explanation: He spends lavishly, reducing his own wealth with only modest, non-strategic benefits.

Event: Big Head decides to share the house with up-and-coming programmers
Explanation: He loses resources and privacy while greatly helping others’ living and working conditions.

Event: Programmers gratefully give Big Head a share of their companies
Explanation: They repay his generosity with equity, creating mutual long-term benefit.

Event: Erlich talks Big Head into forming the VC firm “Bachmanity”
Explanation: Erlich secures funding and control while exposing Big Head to large financial risk.

Event: Big Head gives Erlich access to his bank account
Explanation: He sacrifices nearly all his wealth while Erlich gains full spending power.

Event: Erlich goes on a spending spree with Big Head’s money
Explanation: Erlich enjoys massive consumption while devastating Big Head’s finances.

Event: By the end of the episode, Big Head is completely broke
Explanation: His prior choices culminate in near-total personal financial loss without clear benefit.

Event: Blog Erlich buys triggers events leading to them each owning half of Pied Piper
Explanation: Serendipitously turns wasteful spending into major shared ownership value with no stated harm to others.

Event: Hooli frequently promotes Big Head for publicity reasons
Explanation: Big Head gains status and pay; Hooli gains PR while undermining merit-based structure.

Event: Big Head blows through $20 million on frivolous things
Explanation: He rapidly destroys his own capital for minor lifestyle gains with no benefit to others beyond spending ripple.

Event: Big Head lets Erlich take control of his finances
Explanation: He relinquishes control and is rendered bankrupt, with no upside to himself.

Event: Big Head informs Richard about Hooli using him as a pawn
Explanation: He risks some standing with Hooli while materially helping Richard’s position.

Event: Big Head is consistently pleasant and nice to everyone
Explanation: His disposition builds goodwill and social capital while clearly benefiting others around him.

Event: Richard publicly calls Big Head one of the nicest people he’s met
Explanation: Praise boosts Big Head’s reputation and reinforces pro-social norms.
